title: Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook
wikitext: {{short description|Ireland & England international rugby league footballer}} {{Use dmy dates|date=January 2020}} {{Use British English|date=January 2020}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = Louie "LMS"{{cite web|url=https://www.saintsrlfc.com/2017/09/27/heritage-society-thanks-louie|title=Before the 'Captain's Run' for the Castleford semi-final, there was a special presentation from Saints' Heritage Society to the one-and-only Louie McCarthy Scarsbrook.|publisher=Saints RLFC|date=27 September 2017|accessdate=6 November 2017}} McCarthy Scarsbrook |fullname = Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook |image = Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook Quins.JPG |imagesize = |birth_date = {{birth date and age|df=y|1986|1|14}}{{cite web|title=Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook|url=https://au.sports.yahoo.com/league/player/1885/41/louie-mccarthy-scarsbrook|work=au.sports.yahoo.com|publisher=Yahoo! 7 Sport|accessdate=27 May 2014}} |birth_place = [[Whitechapel]], London, England | height = {{convert|6|ft|2|in|m|abbr=on}}{{cite web|url= http://www.superleague.co.uk/matchday/player/2427|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20140330120000/http://www.superleague.co.uk/matchday/player/2427|url-status= dead|archive-date= 2014-03-30|title= Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook St Helens|author=|date=2015|website= www.superleague.co.uk|publisher= Rugby Football League|access-date=26 July 2015}} |weight = {{convert|16|st|5|lb|kg|abbr=on}} |position = {{Rlp|PR|LF|SR}} |club1 = [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] |year1start = 2006 |year1end = 10 |appearances1 = 97 |tries1 = 20 |goals1 = 0 |fieldgoals1 = 0 |points1 = 70 |club2 = [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] |year2start = 2011 |year2end = 23 |appearances2 = 373 |tries2 = 63 |goals2 = 0 |fieldgoals2 = 0 |points2 = 252 |teamA = [[England national rugby league team|England]] |yearAstart = 2008 |yearAend = 09 |appearancesA = 2 |triesA = 2 |goalsA = 0 |fieldgoalsA = 0 |pointsA = 8 |teamB = [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] |yearBstart = 2017 |yearBend = |appearancesB = 3 |triesB = 1 |goalsB = 0 |fieldgoalsB = 0 |pointsB = 4 |new = yes |source = {{cite web|url=http://www.saints.org.uk/saints.org.uk/home/viewpage.php?page_id=10&num=16713|title=Saints Heritage Society|website=www.saints.org.uk}}{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/louie-mccarthy-scarsbrook/summary.html|title=Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook - Career Stats & Summary - Rugby League Project|first=Shawn Dollin and Andrew|last=Ferguson|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}{{Rugby league records |id=2211 |title=Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook |access-date=7 September 2023}} |updated = 10 October 2023 }} '''Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook''' (born 14 January 1986) is a former [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]]. He has played for both [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] at international level. He came through the London Broncos academy, but only played for the first team when it was named [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] in the [[Super League]]. LMS was contracted to play for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] until 2020, which would be his testimonial season. In close to twelve years, he won four League Leader's Shields in 2014, 2018, 2019 and 2022 plus five Super League championships. He also won the 2021 Challenge Cup with St Helens. ==Background== McCarthy-Scarsbrook was born on 14 January 1986 in [[Whitechapel]], [[London]], England. He grew up on the [[Isle of Dogs]], and was educated at [[St Joseph's Academy, Blackheath]].{{cite web |title=How a scouse-cockney combo can buck trend for St Helens in Grand Final |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/06/st-helens-grand-final-mccarthy-scarsbrook |website=The Guardian |access-date=7 September 2023 |date=6 October 2011}} He played [[association football|football]] as a [[goalkeeper (association football)|goalkeeper]] during his youth, but was persuaded by one of his schoolteachers to try [[rugby league]]. He played for [[Greenwich Admirals]] before joining [[London Broncos]] Academy ranks in 2004.{{cite web |title=Nine picked for Broncos |url=https://www.newsshopper.co.uk/sport/othersport/469821.nine-picked-for-broncos/ |website=News Shopper |access-date=7 September 2023 |date=12 March 2004}} ==Playing career== ===Early career=== McCarthy-Scarsbrook was a product of the [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] Junior Academy squad. He toured Australia with [[British Amateur Rugby League Association]] U18s in 2004. He spent the 2005 season on loan at [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]'s Senior Academy, and played a prominent role in Hull's reserve grade Grand Final winning season.{{cite web |title=What became of Hull FC's 2005 Academy Grand Final winners |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-2005-happened-next-4138296 |website=Hull Daily Mail |access-date=7 September 2023 |date=30 January 2021}} [[File:Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook.jpg|thumb|right|McCarthy-Scarsbrook playing for Harlequins Rugby League]] ===Harlequins RL=== McCarthy-Scarsbrook returned to London and in 2006 made his [[Super League]] début. By 2008 Harlequins coach [[Brian McDermott (rugby league)|Brian McDermott]] was tipping McCarthy-Scarsbrook for a place in [[England national rugby league team|England]]'s [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]] squad.{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/london/7220608.stm |title=Quins ace tipped for England call|publisher=BBC|date=2008-02-05|access-date=2008-02-06|first=Paul|last=Fletcher}}{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/london/7220679.stm |title=Leading the way in London|publisher=BBC|date=2008-02-06|access-date=2008-02-06|first=Paul|last=Fletcher}} McCarthy-Scarsbrook was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].{{cite web|url=http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890 |title=Myler gets England call |publisher=England Rugby League |date=2008-09-29 |accessdate=2008-10-03 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20081007232803/http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890 |archivedate=2008-10-07 }} McCarthy-Scarsbrook was named in the [[England national rugby league team|England]] team to face [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at the [[Keepmoat Stadium]], [[Doncaster]] prior to England's departure for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7642490.stm|title=Gleeson to lead new-look England|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-09|access-date=2008-10-10}} McCarthy-Scarsbrook announced on 1 September 2010{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/london/8961108.stm|title=Prop Louis McCarthy-Scarsbrook to leave Harlequins|publisher=BBC Sport|date=2010-09-01|access-date=2010-01-09}} that he was to leave Quins at the end of the season after turning down a new contract. ===St Helens=== On 3 September 2010, St Helens confirmed the capture of Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook on a four-year deal starting in 2011, where he'll join up with St. Helens' other new signings [[Josh Perry]] and [[Michael Shenton]]. Upon Signing the deal Louie said, '"St Helens are entering an exciting time in their history and to be a part of that was a great pull." He continued: "Not only will they have a new stadium in 2012 but the quality of the players already in the squad means they will be challenging on all fronts. "I am looking forward to linking up with my new teammates and continuing to learn under Royce Simmons. "I would like to thank Quins for giving me the chance to play rugby league and I have enjoyed my time there. The fans and players have been excellent with me and I wish them all well in the future."{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/st_helens/8961108.stm|title=St Helens sign Harlequins' Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook|publisher=BBC Sport|date=2010-09-03|access-date=2010-03-09}} He played in the [[2011 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=Guardian|accessdate=21 November 2019}}{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=Daily Telegraph|accessdate=21 November 2019}} In 2014, McCarthy-Scarsbrook made his 100th appearance for St Helens in their [[2014 Challenge Cup#Fifth round|Challenge Cup]] fixture against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. St Helens reached the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]], and McCarthy-Scarsbrook was selected to play at {{rlp|sr}} in their 14-6 victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/11156623/St-Helens-14-Wigan-Warriors-6-Moment-of-madness-from-Wales-international-Ben-Flower-costs-Wigan-dear.html|title= St Helens 14 Wigan Warriors 6: Moment of madness from Wales international Ben Flower costs Wigan dear|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Daily Telegraph|accessdate=15 October 2014}}{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/11/st-helens-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title= St Helens win Grand Final after Wigan's Ben Flower is sent off|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Guardian |accessdate=15 October 2014}}{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/29559845|title= St Helens 14–6 Wigan Warriors|date= 11 October 2014 |work=BBC Sport|accessdate=15 October 2014}}{{cite news|last1=Cartwright|first1=Phil|title=St Helens v Wigan as it happened|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/live/rugby-league/29416637|accessdate=12 October 2014|work=[[BBC Sport]]|publisher=[[BBC]]|date=11 October 2014}} He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|accessdate=1 November 2019}} He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Salford Red Devils]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|accessdate=25 October 2019}}{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019|last1=Unwin|first1=Will}}{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}} He played in St Helens [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] victory over Wigan at the [[Kingston Communications Stadium]] in Hull.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport}} He played for St. Helens in their [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over Castleford.{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897/|title=How St Helens players have celebrated their first Challenge Cup win in 13 years|website=www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk}} On 9 October 2021, he played for St. Helens in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Catalans Dragons]].{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport}} In round 23 of the 2022 Super League season, McCarthy-Scarsbrook scored a rare double in St Helens 60-6 victory over [[Hull F.C.]]{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/62493296/|title=Super League: Hull FC 6-60 St Helens - Saints confirm semi-final with 11-try demolition|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} On 24 September 2022, McCarthy-Scarsbrook played off the interchange bench in St Helens 24-12 victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the 2022 Super League Grand Final.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|website=www.theguardian.com}} On 18 February 2023, McCarthy-Scarsbrook played in St Helens 13-12 upset victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] in the [[2023 World Club Challenge]].{{cite news |title=St Helens stun Penrith to win World Club Challenge |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64666651 |work=BBC Sport}} On 20 September 2023, McCarthy-Scarsbrook announced he would be retiring from rugby league.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66870327|title=Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook: St Helens stalwart to retire after 18 seasons|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} He played 26 games for St Helens in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished third on the table. He played in St Helens narrow loss against the Catalans Dragons in the semi-final which stopped them reaching a fifth successive grand final. This would also be McCarthy-Scarsbrook's final game as a player.{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/23856044.paul-wellens-q-saints-review-year-look-2024/|title=Paul Wellens Q&A: Saints review of year and a look to 2024|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk}} ===International career=== McCarthy-Scarsbrook made his [[England national rugby league team|England]] début in the victory over [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] on 10 October 2008.{{cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_4297504,00.html|title=England warm up in style|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-10-10|accessdate=11 October 2008}} His second game for England was also against Wales, a 48-12 victory in the [[2009 Four Nations tournament]] in which he scored a try. He later switched allegiance to [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]], and was named in their [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]] squad.{{cite web|url=http://www.sbs.com.au/news/article/2017/10/07/ireland-name-rugby-league-world-cup-squad |title=Ireland name Rugby League World Cup squad |publisher=sbs.com.au |date=7 October 2017 |accessdate=7 October 2017}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.saintsrlfc.com/teams/first-team/louie-mccarthy-scarsbrook/ St Helens profile] *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20090213144345/http://www.league.quins.co.uk/squad/index.php?player=81342&includeref=dynamic Quins RL profile] *[https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2007/jun/05/rugbyleague.sport1 Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook, Harlequins] *[http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3092429,00.html Quins targeting top six] *[https://www.superleague.co.uk/player/14/louie%3Cbr-%3Emccarthy-scarsbrook SL profile] *[http://www.rlwc2017.com/players?team=427#Louie-McCarthy-Scarsbrook Statistics at rlwc2017.com] *[http://www.saints.org.uk/saints/player.php?num=16713 Saints Heritage Society profile] {{Navboxes |title = St Helens |titlestyle = background: white |list = {{St Helens squad -
